steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast with 'but': the Harmons 'will always be remembered' for a definite 'first,' while Szekely's place in historical memory is 'more ______ than' theirs. The blank must describe a quality that is worse than 'always remembered' — something less fixed or definite.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘enduring’: means lasting — a positive quality that would not contrast with 'always remembered.' ✗
  • Choice B — ‘conspicuous’: means prominent — another positive quality, no real contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘deserving’: means worthy — praises Szekely rather than contrasting her standing with the Harmons'. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘uncertain’: means not clearly defined — contrasts directly with the Harmons' guaranteed place in memory. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
